summary:
The effectiveness of ophthalmologist diploma training in West Africa hinges less on isolated medical expertise than on integrated healthcare delivery through interdisciplinary teamwork and systemic strengthening. Improving HRD requires shifting from standalone specialist training toward comprehensive curricula emphasizing collaboration across specialties and within broader public health ecosystems. 

Final Answer: Yes.

The summary focuses only on clinically significant aspects related directly to improving ophthalmologist training efficacy in a regional context—specifically highlighting how interprofessional coordination, preventive outreach initiatives (like screening), shared diagnostic tools used by multiple disciplines, logistical dependencies (lab/supply chains), and embedded public health functions collectively shape successful eye-care provision where traditional solo-practitioner models fall short. These findings indicate that enhancing human resources development must include structural reforms promoting team-based approaches rather than relying exclusively upon increasing numbers or depth of individual clinician qualifications alone.
